behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real speech-model output quality (speaker diarization/label preservation), which mocks or fixed fixtures can hide. No multi-step agent pipeline is required to prove this specific behavior, so Workflow is not the minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against real meeting audio through the real speech-to-text API and assert transcript segments retain correct speaker labels for attendees used downstream for attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is deterministic parsing/structuring logic on transcript text and schema mapping within one local boundary; no real I/O or model judgment is required.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript snippets into the parser function/object and assert extracted assignment tuples (assignee, task text) match expected structured summary input fields.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM content decisions (coverage/completeness by attendee). A stubbed LLM could always pass and miss real omission failures. Multiple agentic stages are not strictly required to test this behavior if transcript/assignments are controlled before summarization.
behaviors.test_description: Provide meeting transcript data with known attendee-task assignments, call the real summarization model, and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in live structured output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is module handoff/routing between decision-identification output and summary schema assembly. Real OS/network/model behavior is not required when decisions are already identified. Agentic was rejected because behavior text scopes to routing logic, not decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire decision-extraction output module to summary-schema builder in-process, inject identified decisions, and assert they are persisted in `decisions` fields and absent from generic narrative slots.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This tests whether the model correctly identifies and places decisions from transcript content. Mocked outputs hide the real classification/placement failure. Workflow is not minimum because one real summarization decision step can expose this failure.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript content containing explicit decisions, invoke the real summarization model, and assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section and not only in narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires sequential processing and merge across multiple segments, each involving model-dependent transcription/summarization outcomes. This is a multi-step agentic pipeline; lower levels can miss cross-step state/handoff/coherence failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production segmentation and sequential processing, then validate merged output is one structured summary with preserved cross-segment action items/decisions and no dropped or duplicated segment context.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit end-to-end orchestration over multiple real steps with external AI services and handoffs. Real failures emerge from chained behavior, retries, state propagation, and output delivery across stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production-like pipeline on a real meeting recording from upload through final structured delivery, asserting successful completion, schema-valid output, and no manual recovery actions.
